title: A Childhood Memory
summary: A narrator shares a warm memory of spending summers at her grandmother's farmhouse as a child, describing the sights, smells, and simple pleasures that made those days unforgettable.
body: When I was a child, I spent every summer at my grandmother's farmhouse in the countryside. The house was surrounded by green fields and tall trees, and there was always a wonderful smell of fresh bread coming from the kitchen. My grandmother woke up early every morning to bake, and by the time I came downstairs, the table was already set with warm rolls, butter, and homemade jam. After breakfast, I would run outside with my cousins and we would explore the fields and the old barn at the back of the property. We made up stories about hidden treasure and pretended to be explorers. In the evenings, my grandmother would sit in her favourite armchair and tell us stories about when she was young. Her voice was soft and slow, and her stories were full of funny characters and surprising endings. I can still remember the sound of the crickets through the open window and the smell of the garden after rain. Those summers felt long and peaceful. Now that I am older, I understand how lucky I was. Simple moments like those are the ones we carry with us for the rest of our lives.

annotations.exampleEn: The old castle is surrounded by a wide moat.
A delicious smell was coming from the kitchen.
When I came downstairs, everyone was already eating.
The children made up stories about dragons and wizards.
He pretended to be a doctor and gave his toys check-ups.
My grandfather always reads in his favourite armchair.

annotations.meaningVi: đầy những nhân vật buồn cười
annotations.exampleEn: The book was full of funny characters and silly situations.
We fell asleep to the sound of crickets outside.
The holiday felt long and peaceful, with nothing to rush.

annotations.meaningVi: mang theo (trong ký ức) suốt đời
annotations.exampleEn: Some childhood experiences we carry with us all our lives.

annotations.meaningVi: phần còn lại của cuộc đời
annotations.exampleEn: She promised to remember that moment for the rest of her life.

grammarNotes.title: 'Would' for repeated past habits
grammarNotes.bodyVi: Dùng 'would + verb nguyên thể' để diễn tả những thói quen hoặc hành động lặp lại trong quá khứ. Nó tương tự 'used to' nhưng thiên về hành động hơn là trạng thái. Ví dụ: 'I would run outside with my cousins.' / 'My grandmother would sit in her armchair.' Dạng này thường gặp trong văn kể chuyện về ký ức.
grammarNotes.bodyEn: Use 'would + base verb' to describe repeated past habits or routines. It is similar to 'used to' but focuses on actions, not states. Examples: 'I would run outside every day.', 'She would tell us stories each evening.' This form is common in nostalgic storytelling.

grammarNotes.title: Time clauses with 'by the time' and 'when'
grammarNotes.bodyVi: 'When + mệnh đề' chỉ thời điểm xảy ra hành động: 'When I was a child...' 'By the time + mệnh đề' nhấn mạnh rằng một việc đã hoàn thành trước khi việc khác xảy ra: 'By the time I came downstairs, the table was already set.' Đây là cách diễn đạt thứ tự thời gian rất tự nhiên trong tiếng Anh.
grammarNotes.bodyEn: 'When + clause' introduces the time of an action: 'When I came downstairs...' 'By the time + clause' shows that one action was already complete before another: 'By the time I came downstairs, the table was set.' Both are common in narratives about the past.

translationVi: Khi còn nhỏ, tôi dành mỗi mùa hè ở nhà trang trại của bà ngoại tôi ở vùng quê. Ngôi nhà bao quanh bởi những cánh đồng xanh và những cây cao, và luôn có mùi thơm tuyệt vời của bánh mì tươi toả ra từ căn bếp. Bà ngoại tôi thức dậy sớm mỗi buổi sáng để nướng bánh, và đến khi tôi đi xuống cầu thang, bàn đã được dọn sẵn với bánh mì nóng, bơ và mứt tự làm. Sau bữa sáng, tôi sẽ chạy ra ngoài với các anh chị em họ và chúng tôi sẽ khám phá những cánh đồng và cái kho cũ ở phía sau khu đất. Chúng tôi bịa những câu chuyện về kho báu ẩn giấu và giả vờ là những nhà thám hiểm. Vào buổi tối, bà ngoại sẽ ngồi trên chiếc ghế bành yêu thích của bà và kể cho chúng tôi nghe những câu chuyện về khi bà còn trẻ. Giọng bà nhẹ nhàng và chậm rãi, và những câu chuyện của bà đầy những nhân vật buồn cười và kết thúc bất ngờ. Tôi vẫn nhớ tiếng dế kêu qua cửa sổ mở và mùi khu vườn sau mưa. Những mùa hè đó cảm giác dài và bình yên. Bây giờ khi tôi lớn hơn, tôi hiểu mình đã may mắn như thế nào. Những khoảnh khắc giản dị như vậy là những khoảnh khắc chúng ta mang theo suốt phần còn lại của cuộc đời.

questions.prompt: Where did the narrator spend her summers as a child?
options: At her aunt's house in the city
questions.explanation: The narrator says 'I spent every summer at my grandmother's farmhouse in the countryside.'
questions.hint: A family member's home in the countryside is mentioned.

options: A large garden and a pond
questions.explanation: The narrator says 'The house was surrounded by green fields and tall trees.'

questions.prompt: What could the narrator smell when she woke up?
questions.explanation: The narrator says there was 'a wonderful smell of fresh bread coming from the kitchen.'
questions.hint: The smell came from something her grandmother baked.

questions.prompt: What was on the table when the narrator came downstairs?
options: Warm rolls, butter, and homemade jam
questions.explanation: The narrator says 'the table was already set with warm rolls, butter, and homemade jam.'

questions.prompt: Where did the narrator and her cousins explore after breakfast?
options: The fields and the old barn
questions.explanation: The narrator says they 'explore the fields and the old barn at the back of the property.'
questions.hint: One outdoor area and one old building are mentioned.

questions.prompt: What did the children pretend to be when they played outside?
questions.explanation: The narrator says 'we pretended to be explorers.'
questions.hint: They made up stories about hidden treasure and acted out a role.

questions.prompt: Where did the grandmother sit in the evenings?
questions.explanation: The narrator says 'my grandmother would sit in her favourite armchair.'
questions.hint: It is a specific type of comfortable chair.

questions.prompt: How does the narrator describe her grandmother's voice?
questions.explanation: The narrator says 'Her voice was soft and slow.'

questions.prompt: What did the narrator notice through the open window at night?
questions.explanation: The narrator says 'I can still remember the sound of the crickets through the open window.'
questions.hint: It is a natural sound made by small insects.

questions.prompt: How does the narrator describe those summer days?
questions.explanation: The narrator says 'Those summers felt long and peaceful.'
questions.hint: Two adjectives describe the feeling of those days.

questions.prompt: What lesson does the narrator share at the end?
options: Nature is more beautiful than city life
Simple moments are the ones we carry with us for the rest of our lives
Children should spend more time outdoors
questions.explanation: The narrator says 'Simple moments like those are the ones we carry with us for the rest of our lives.'
questions.hint: The final sentence contains the narrator's reflection.

cloze.template: When I was a child, I spent every summer at my grandmother's farmhouse in the {{b1}}. The house was surrounded by green fields and tall trees, and there was always a wonderful smell of fresh {{b2}} coming from the kitchen. My grandmother woke up early every morning to bake, and by the time I came {{b3}}, the table was already set with warm rolls, butter, and homemade jam. After breakfast, I would run outside with my cousins and we would explore the fields and the old {{b4}} at the back of the property. We made up stories about hidden {{b5}} and pretended to be explorers. In the evenings, my grandmother would sit in her favourite {{b6}} and tell us stories about when she was young. Her voice was soft and slow, and her stories were full of funny characters and surprising {{b7}}. I can still remember the sound of the {{b8}} through the open window and the smell of the garden after rain. Those summers felt long and {{b9}}. Now that I am older, I understand how {{b10}} I was. Simple moments like those are the ones we carry with us for the rest of our lives.

blanks.explanation: 'countryside' — the farmhouse was located in the countryside, away from the city.
'bread' — the grandmother baked bread, and its smell filled the house.
'downstairs' — the narrator came downstairs from the bedroom to find breakfast ready.
'barn' — an old barn at the back of the farm was one of their favourite places to explore.
'treasure' — the children made up stories about hidden treasure.
'armchair' — a comfortable chair with armrests; the grandmother's favourite seat.
'endings' — the grandmother's stories had surprising endings.
'crickets' — insects that make a chirping sound at night.
'peaceful' — the narrator describes those summers as long and peaceful.
'lucky' — the narrator now understands how lucky she was to have those summers.

criticalThinkingQuestion: The narrator says 'simple moments are the ones we carry with us for the rest of our lives.' Do you agree that simple everyday moments often create our most lasting memories? Why might simple experiences stay with us more than big, expensive events?
